scripts_added.purpose: Collapse Step 1 (build) + Step 2 (install) + Step 3 (launch) + Step 4 (launch screenshot) into a single invocation with argument-driven configuration.
steps: boot named simulator (idempotent; tolerates already-booted state)
xcrun simctl io booted screenshot -> $EVIDENCE_DIR/ios-screenshot-launch.png (verifies non-empty)
scripts_added.style_notes: Follows scripts/benchmark/score-project.sh conventions: set -euo pipefail, '[ios-runner] step N ok: ...' diagnostic echo per phase, non-zero exit on any failure, tee to evidence logs.

skill_md_edits.change: Added 'Fast path' paragraph immediately under '## Step 2: Install' heading pointing at scripts/ios-runner.sh

verification.inline_steps_retained: all 9 original steps remain in SKILL.md; fast-path is additive

notes: Script is 159 lines, ~40 over the 100-120 target because of the named-arg parser, required-arg validation, idempotent boot handling, and the showBuildSettings-based .app resolution (which is more robust than `find build/ -name '*.app'`).
Uses ${!req} indirect expansion to validate required args uniformly; requires bash 3.2+ which ships on macOS.
Screenshot size check (-s) prevents 0-byte screenshot evidence from silently passing.
Launch PID extraction is best-effort — simctl's output format is 'com.example.app: 12345' — if parsing fails, step still logs 'unknown' rather than failing.
